criteria.criterion: Bootstrap getMe no longer warns on Windows when PowerShell can validate the same token after a Node HTTPS timeout.
criteria.summary: The transport-success path is locked by automated helper and install-runtime tests. The live host still reproduces the original Node HTTPS timeout, and the updated token stored in the isolated worktree .env now validates @Gerta_homebot through the PowerShell-assisted fallback, while the earlier source .env 401 artifacts remain as a negative control for invalid credentials.

criteria.criterion: Bot sendMessage no longer drops outbound replies on Windows when Node HTTPS fails but PowerShell Bot API requests succeed.
criteria.summary: The bot runtime now retries sendMessage through Windows PowerShell only when Node HTTPS transport throws, and dedicated bot tests prove the fallback succeeds without hiding Telegram HTTP responses.

criteria.criterion: Regression coverage proves the Windows fallback path without masking invalid-token or Bot API HTTP failures.
criteria.summary: Scoped bootstrap/bot tests, full repo build/lint/typecheck/test, bundle validation, and live success-plus-failure artifacts all show the product now distinguishes transport recovery from invalid-token failure instead of flattening everything into a network warning.
